steps.title: Use a modifier, not a clause
steps.content: The opening phrase modifies the subject it in the main clause. Before that main clause, the sentence needs a nonfinite participial phrase, not a separate finite verb.

steps.title: Reject finite verbs
steps.content:
• Choice A uses a finite present-tense verb without a subject in the opening phrase.
  • Choice B uses a finite past-perfect verb in the same ungrammatical position.
  • Choice D uses a finite past-tense verb before the main clause.

steps.title: Use the participle
steps.content: Written forms the participial phrase Originally written in ancient Greek, which correctly modifies the poem before the main clause. The answer is C.
